emergent_themes.description: Pattern of building full carts but abandoning orders at checkout due to overthinking and financial anxiety
Social context providing permission to override individual financial caution and spend on indulgent orders
Shift in perception of time value during university progression, leading to increased convenience spending
Tension between purely functional individual food attitudes and strong preference for social eating experiences

coding_summary: P036 reveals a complex relationship with food delivery characterized by decision paralysis (frequently building carts but abandoning orders), a purely functional approach to cooking that contrasts with strong social eating preferences, and a significant behavioral shift during first year from self-reliant to convenience-seeking. Key patterns include social influence overriding financial caution, promotional sensitivity driving ordering behavior, and evolving time-value perceptions. Her cart abandonment behavior and subscription psychology provide unique insights into student decision-making processes.
